title: Boat Rental in Sporades: A Day on the Water in 2026
html:
The Sporades archipelago in the Aegean Sea offers some of Greece's finest cruising grounds. Setting off from Skiathos' central port or Skopelos' harbour, you can explore turquoise coves and pine-clad islands for a half-day or longer. Prices for a shared tour start around €60 per person, while a private motorboat hire costs from €250. BoatHire24 connects you with verified boat owners across the region.

Where to set off in Sporades

The main departure points are Skiathos New Marina, Skopelos Harbour, Alonissos Old Port, and the smaller pier at Glossa on Skopelos. From Skiathos you can reach Lalaria Beach in 20 minutes. Skopelos Town is a good base for exploring the northern islands. Many skippered charters in Sporades depart from these harbours, and skippered charters in Sporades are a popular option for less experienced sailors.

Which boat to rent

  1. Rib or small motorboat, Ideal for hugging the coast and reaching secluded beaches; carries up to 6 people; from €250 half-day.
  2. Catamaran, Stable family cruising with ample deck space; up to 10 guests; from €800 full-day.
  3. Motor yacht (e.g. Prestige 500), Luxury and speed for island-hopping; up to 8 guests; from €1,200 per day. Visit the Prestige 500 charter page for availability.
  4. Sailing yacht, Classic experience with helming fun; up to 6 guests; from €500 per day.
  5. Gulet, Traditional wooden motor-sailer for large parties; up to 20 guests; from €2,000 per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Sporades?

Expect to pay between €60 and €3,000 per day, depending on boat type and season. Here is a quick price-band breakdown:

  • Shared island tour: €60, €90 per person (half-day).
  • Small motorboat (without skipper) for half-day: from €250.
  • Catamaran full-day (with skipper): €800, €1,600.
  • Luxury crewed motor yacht (full-day): €1,200, €3,000.

Prices quoted in EUR for 2026. Most operators offer discounts for multi-day bookings.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Sporades?

For boats over 30hp (typically over 6m), Greek law requires a recognised powerboat licence or ICC. However, many rental companies provide licenced skippers so you can charter without your own. The operator will confirm exact requirements when you book; it varies by boat size. A local tip: the Meltemi wind funnels down from the north in July and August, so check forecast if you are helming yourself.

If you plan to anchor overnight, pack a second set of fenders, the mooring buoys in Alonissos Marine Park can be rough on gelcoat. Also, carry cash for taverna-only islands like Kyra Panagia.

When is the best time to rent a boat in Sporades?

The high season runs from June to September, with July and August being the hottest and busiest months. The prevailing wind is the Meltemi, which is strongest in August. Shoulder months (May and October) are calmer, cheaper, and still warm. Book well ahead for peak July, August charters; and be aware that weekly charters count a week as 7 nights. Prices drop by roughly 20% outside July and August.

At a glance

  • Price range: €60, €3,000 per day
  • Peak months: July, August
  • Typical trip length: half-day or full-day (weekly charters also available)
  • Max capacity: up to 20 guests on a gulet / 6, 10 on a motor yacht
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Sporades

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Sporades?

Yes, you can hire a small boat under 30hp (usually 4, 6 metres) without a licence, as long as you have some experience. For larger boats, you need a licence or you can hire a skipper through the operator.

How many nautical miles from Skiathos to Skopelos?

The distance between Skiathos New Marina and Skopelos Town is about 15 nautical miles, a comfortable 1-hour cruise at moderate speed.

Are there hidden extras like fuel and cleaning fees?

Most listed boats include basic fuel for a standard day trip, but you should confirm with the owner. A cleaning fee of around €50 may apply for a full-day charter; this is usually stated in the listing.

Can I take the boat to a different island for lunch?

Yes, all charter boats allow you to explore freely within the agreed area. Lunch spots like Stafilos beach on Skopelos or the waterfront tavernas of Alonissos are popular choices.

Whether you plan a self-drive adventure or a crewed luxury escape, the Sporades deliver unforgettable days on the water. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Sporades or list your own. If you own a boat here, you can become a host and earn from your craft when you are not using it.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Sporades and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
